name: Graphic Autonumber
description:
•	Graphics Tracker is the Primary tool for Pursuit Services graphics support on Word response documents which is used for submitting and tracking progress of graphic requests, and inventory management for future reference/reuse. It is also used for storage of completed graphic files, associated templates, and production materials

•	Review RFX/project scope, design & submission requirements
•	Consult with deal team to gather and assess client insights and win themes
•	Discuss and brainstorm with deal team for materials to support submission/meeting
•	Create document templates complying with client and RFX requirements/branding
•	Leverage repository of reusable graphics/assets to create custom graphics
•	Use the Graphics tracker to track status for word only
•	Create graphics tracker to track status of individual graphics files
•	Update graphics tracker where graphics modifications requested - new slide within the file is created for each modification
•	Paste completed graphics files to Word file
•	Use iterative review cycle to refine graphics based on input from deal team
•	Format final documents and conduct quality check
•	Prepare files for final print production or digital submission
•	Confirm all final files have been uploaded for archiving/harvesting
scope:
In-Scope Activities
Activity/ Scenario	Location/ Geography	FTEs (Production FTEs)	Operating Window
Automate the Graphic Control number using the following naming convention: Opp ID, Year and Order	All Markets	262	Windows
objectives:
This section to cover all relevant metrics used to gauge the process performance for all activities in scope

Process Performance	
Activity	Average Monthly Volume (6 Months)	Average Handling Time	Seasonal peak Volume if applicable	Turnaround Time	Any other SLA
	FTEs	Additional Comments (Kick off and Cut-off time)
Pursuit Services - Graphic Tracker Autonumbering	58050 approx.	2 mins/per graphic	N/A	2 mins/per graphic	N/A	224	N/A

The above table is indicative only and actual timelines may vary from deal to deal. Also, we expect the volume handled to increase by 10-20% in FY25.
criteria:
•	Draft Graphic request is submitted on the tracker.
•	All Graphic must be modified in some way to be incorporated into the proposal response and must be logged for tracking purposes.
•	A notification can be sent to the designer that a new item has been added to the tracker. 
•	The designer updates the tracker that the Graphic is In Progress.
•	Designer notifies the submitter that this is now completed, reviewer will review and update status of Graphic Tracker Request Item if any edits or changes needed
•	Graphic tracker is updated to complete status once received confirmation from requestor that this can be incorporated into the proposal response and no further edits are required.

1.text:
Order is the unique graphic number generated by the Graphics Tracker. This number will always be four numeric characters (e.g., 0001, 0999) 

Title  : ABC
	
Graphic Control Number : 000007.26.0001 

000007 is opportunity ID, 26 is the year like 2026, 0001 is the order

2.text: Its not editable once generated it's auto populate everywhere in read only mode

3.text: The Graphic Tracker Autonumbering Power App is built on Microsoft Power Platform and integrates with Microsoft Teams, Dataverse and SharePoint

4.text: No peak hours spikes will be there because only 300 max users will be using the app and even if there is more users using application will not impact performance as we have simple action items by generating numbers we are sending emails for it.we dont have complicated logic or api's we are calling here

5.text: This numbers are autogenerated so failure scenario will  not come this can be happen only if power automate plateform will be down and below 1 business rule we have : - Team and Channel dropdowns display only Teams/Channels user is part of

6.text: No its not required as it is very simple and straightforward application

7.text:
After successful run below email will trigger and its the indicate of successful process:Email Notifications – In edit list, user can have option to toggle between email notifications as on/off to receive email notifications whenever there is a status change with that list item to the author. The field will be a toggle and will be called “Email status updates to Author”. 

  

Dear Author/SME, 
 
We are pleased to inform you that the graphic for your proposal has been completed. Below are the details of the completed graphic: 

Title : ABC

Graphic Control Number : 000007.26.0001  

Author/SME/Contact  : Name 

Submitted By : GraphicTrackerAutonu 

If you have any questions or need further assistance, please feel free to reach out to the assigned Graphic Designer. 
 
If you require any modifications to the completed graphics, you can edit the status of the graphic to “Status.” Please provide the necessary details and instructions for the modifications through this link - Go to the tracker 
 
Thank you. 
 
Note: This is an automatically generated email, please do not reply.

changeRequests.revisionNotes: Not able to download approved final PDD from the artifacts section, so attaching the PDD approved by CCB.

baGaps.question: What is the exact format and structure of the autonumber (e.g., delimiter, padding, character length for Opp ID/Year/Order)? Should the 'Order' be sequential per opportunity, per year, or globally, and how should collisions or re-runs be prevented when graphics are modified (where the PDD states 'a new slide within the file is created for each modification')?

How should the autonumber behave for modified/revised graphics versus net-new graphics? Does a modification inherit the parent autonumber with a version suffix, or is a brand new control number issued? The success criteria mention iterative review cycles but do not clarify versioning rules.

What is the integration approach with the existing Graphics Tracker tool? Is this a plugin/macro within the tracker, an external service, or a replacement module? What APIs, data schemas, or file formats (Excel, SharePoint list, database) does the current tracker expose for read/write of the control number field?

Given the volume (~58,050 graphics/month) and a 10-20% expected growth in FY25, what are the throughput, concurrency, and latency requirements for the autonumbering service? Are there peak-hour spikes (e.g., end-of-quarter pursuit deadlines) that require burst capacity beyond the stated 2-minute average handling time?

What validation rules and exception handling are required when the Opportunity ID is missing, malformed, duplicated across markets, or when a graphic request spans multiple opportunities? Who is the business owner for resolving these exceptions, and what is the fallback if the autonumber service is unavailable?

What audit trail, logging, and reporting capabilities are required for compliance and harvesting/archiving (referenced in the success criteria)? Specifically, who can view, modify, or override an assigned autonumber, and what RBAC model applies across the 262 in-scope FTEs across all markets?

The success criteria mention notifications to designer/submitter/reviewer at multiple status transitions, but the scope only covers autonumbering. Is notification orchestration in-scope for this automation, or is it handled by the existing Graphics Tracker? If in-scope, what channels (email, Teams, in-app) and SLAs apply?

sddDocument.overview: The Graphic Tracker Autonumbering system is a Microsoft Power Platform application that automates the generation of unique graphic control numbers (format: OppID.YY.Order, e.g., 000007.26.0001) for the Pursuit Services graphics workflow. Built on Power Apps with Dataverse as the data store and integrated with Microsoft Teams and SharePoint, it serves approximately 300 concurrent users across 262 FTEs globally, processing ~58,050 graphic requests monthly. The system auto-generates read-only control numbers upon graphic request submission and triggers email notifications to authors/SMEs upon status changes, replacing manual numbering with deterministic, collision-free assignment.

architectureStyle.rationale: Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps + Power Automate + Dataverse) is inherently a low-code serverless architecture. Given the simple business logic (number generation + email notifications), modest user base (300 max concurrent users), absence of complex APIs or computational workloads, and the stated requirement that BT confirmed 'we don't have complicated logic or APIs we are calling here', a serverless low-code platform eliminates infrastructure management, provides built-in scaling for the 58K/month volume, and natively integrates with the Microsoft 365 ecosystem already used by Pursuit Services.

components.name: Graphic Tracker Power App (Canvas/Model-driven)
components.responsibility: User-facing interface for submitting graphic requests, viewing tracker entries, editing request metadata, toggling email notification preferences, and displaying the auto-generated read-only Graphic Control Number. Enforces business rule that Team/Channel dropdowns show only Teams/Channels the user belongs to.

components.name: Autonumber Generation Flow (Power Automate)
components.responsibility: Triggered on new graphic request creation. Constructs the control number by concatenating Opportunity ID (6-digit), 2-digit year, and a 4-digit sequential order number scoped per opportunity. Writes the resulting read-only value back to the Dataverse record. Guarantees uniqueness via Dataverse transactional sequence lookup.

components.name: Dataverse Data Layer
components.responsibility: Primary system of record for graphic request entities, status, metadata, opportunity references, and the immutable Graphic Control Number field. Hosts the per-opportunity sequence counter table used to allocate the next Order value atomically.

components.name: Status Notification Flow (Power Automate)
components.responsibility: Triggered on status change events when 'Email status updates to Author' toggle is enabled. Composes and sends the standardized completion email containing Title, Graphic Control Number, Author/SME, and tracker link to the request author/SME.

components.name: Microsoft Teams Integration
components.responsibility: Embeds the Power App within Teams channels for in-context access by Pursuit Services FTEs. Surfaces only Teams/Channels the authenticated user is a member of in app dropdowns.

components.name: SharePoint Document Library
components.responsibility: Stores completed graphic files, templates, and production materials. Linked to Dataverse records via the Graphic Control Number for archiving and future harvesting/reuse.

components.name: Azure AD / Microsoft Entra ID
components.responsibility: Authenticates all 262 FTE users via existing Accenture single sign-on. Provides identity context to enforce Teams/Channels visibility and Power Platform security roles.

sddDocument.dataFlow: A submitter opens the Graphic Tracker Power App from within Microsoft Teams and authenticates via Azure AD SSO. They fill in graphic request fields (Title, Opportunity ID, Team, Channel, Author/SME) and submit. The app creates a new record in Dataverse, which triggers the Autonumber Generation Power Automate flow. The flow reads the Opportunity ID, derives the 2-digit year, and atomically increments the per-opportunity counter in a sequence table to allocate the next 4-digit Order. It writes the concatenated Graphic Control Number (e.g., 000007.26.0001) back to the Dataverse record as a read-only field, auto-populating it everywhere. Designers see the new request in their tracker view, update status as they progress (Pending → In Progress → Completed). On each status transition, the Status Notification Flow checks the author's email toggle preference and, if enabled, sends a templated email via Outlook connector containing the Graphic Control Number and a link back to the tracker. Completed graphic files are stored in SharePoint, indexed by the control number for archival and reuse.

techStack.frontend: Microsoft Power Apps (Canvas App embedded in Teams) — low-code, native Teams integration, no separate hosting required, aligns with BT's confirmed platform choice and reduces development time for the simple form-based UX.
techStack.backend: Power Automate cloud flows — serverless workflow engine for autonumber generation and email orchestration. No custom backend code required, matches the 'simple action items' scope confirmed by BT.
techStack.database: Microsoft Dataverse — managed relational data store with built-in security, audit, transactional integrity for atomic sequence increment, and native Power Platform integration. Stores graphic request entities, sequence counters, and status history.
techStack.infrastructure: Microsoft Power Platform (SaaS) hosted in Accenture's existing Microsoft 365 / Azure tenant. No infrastructure provisioning needed; scaling, patching, and HA are managed by Microsoft. Premium Power Platform licenses required for the 300 active users to access Dataverse and premium connectors.

integrations.purpose: Host the Power App for in-context user access; restrict Team/Channel dropdown values to user's memberships
Primary data persistence for graphic requests, control numbers, and sequence counters
Store completed graphic files, templates, and production assets linked by control number for archival and harvesting
Send templated status-change notification emails to authors/SMEs
User authentication and identity for membership-based dropdown filtering

nonFunctional.scalability: Power Platform scales elastically within Microsoft-managed capacity. With BT-confirmed ceiling of ~300 concurrent users and ~58K records/month (~2K/business day, ~250/hour peak), the workload sits well below Dataverse and Power Automate throttling limits. Per-opportunity sequence counters distribute write contention, avoiding hotspots. The 10-20% FY25 growth is absorbed without architectural changes.
nonFunctional.security: Authentication via Azure AD SSO leveraging Accenture's existing identity. Authorization via Power Platform security roles aligned to the 262 FTE base. The Graphic Control Number is enforced as read-only post-generation at the Dataverse field level, preventing user override. Teams/Channels visibility filter prevents data leakage across pursuits. Data resides in Accenture's Microsoft 365 tenant under existing data residency and compliance controls.
nonFunctional.performance: Target average handling time aligns with the PDD-stated 2 minutes per graphic, dominated by user input rather than system processing. Autonumber generation completes in sub-second (single Dataverse transaction). Email notification dispatch occurs asynchronously and does not block UX. No peak-hour spikes are anticipated per BT confirmation, so no burst capacity provisioning required.
nonFunctional.reliability: Inherits Microsoft Power Platform SLA (99.9% for Dataverse and Power Automate). Atomic sequence allocation in Dataverse prevents duplicate control numbers under concurrent submissions. Power Automate flow run history provides retry visibility for transient failures. BT acknowledged that platform outage is the only failure mode; in that case, request submission queues until service restoration — no custom fallback designed since BT confirmed it is not required.

risks.risk: BT did not address audit trail and RBAC override governance (Q6). Without an explicit audit log of who created/viewed records and a defined override policy, compliance gaps may emerge during archival/harvesting reviews across the 262-FTE multi-market user base.
risks.mitigation: Enable Dataverse auditing on the graphic request table at minimum for create/update/delete operations. Document the implicit 'no override' policy (control number is system-generated and read-only) in operational runbooks, and revisit with BT before go-live if compliance teams raise concerns.

risks.risk: BT did not clarify versioning rules for modified graphics (Q2 partially answered). The PDD describes 'a new slide within the file is created for each modification,' but the response only confirms the number is read-only once generated. It is unclear whether each modification triggers a new control number or reuses the parent.
risks.mitigation: Confirm during build phase with BT whether modifications create a new tracker record (and therefore a new autonumber) or update the existing record. Default to one-control-number-per-tracker-record and document the assumption explicitly in the LLD.

risks.risk: Sequence collision risk under high-concurrency submissions for the same Opportunity ID if the autonumber flow does not use atomic Dataverse operations.
risks.mitigation: Implement the sequence increment using a Dataverse plugin or a Power Automate flow with optimistic concurrency / row-level locking on the counter table. Add a unique constraint on the Graphic Control Number column as a defense-in-depth backstop.

risks.risk: Premium Power Platform licensing cost for 300 users (Dataverse + premium connectors) may exceed budget assumptions if not validated upfront.
risks.mitigation: Confirm licensing model (per-user vs. per-app plan) with Accenture's Microsoft licensing team during architecture sign-off; consider per-app plans if users only access this single app.

risks.risk: Power Platform tenant-level API throttling could affect throughput during simultaneous bulk submissions (e.g., end-of-quarter pursuit deadlines), despite BT's assertion that no peaks occur.
risks.mitigation: Configure Power Automate concurrency control and monitor flow run analytics for the first 90 days post-launch. Establish alerting on throttle events and revisit capacity if observed.

risks.risk: Year rollover (e.g., 26 → 27) requires the sequence counter to reset semantics per opportunity; ambiguity could cause the Order to continue from the prior year instead of restarting.
risks.mitigation: Design the sequence table with composite key (OpportunityID + Year) so that each year-opportunity pair maintains its own counter, ensuring 0001 is allocated for the first graphic of each new year per opportunity.

risks.risk: PDD content delivered to this design phase contained corrupted/binary segments, suggesting incomplete requirements transfer. Details such as full status workflow states, edit permissions, and exception handling may be missing.
risks.mitigation: Request a clean, fully readable PDD before Tech Lead LLD decomposition; validate all status transitions and field-level rules with BT in a follow-up session prior to development kickoff.

tddDocument.summary: This TDD decomposes the Graphic Tracker Autonumbering serverless Power Platform solution into atomic build tasks across Dataverse schema, Power Automate flows, Canvas Power App, Teams/SharePoint integration, and operational hardening. Implementation follows the SDD's low-code Microsoft 365 stack with Dataverse as system of record, Power Automate for autonumber generation and email notifications, and Power Apps embedded in Teams for the UX. Each task is scoped for a single developer (Power Platform maker) to complete in under 3 days.

modules.name: Dataverse Schema
modules.purpose: Owns the relational data model: graphic request table, per-opportunity sequence counter table, status reference data, and the read-only Graphic Control Number column.

interfaces.name: gt_graphicrequest
interfaces.signature: Entity { gt_title: string(200), gt_opportunityid: string(6), gt_year: int, gt_order: int, gt_graphiccontrolnumber: string(14) [unique, read-only], gt_authorsmeid: lookup(systemuser), gt_teamid: string, gt_channelid: string, gt_status: optionset(Pending|InProgress|Completed), gt_emailtoauthor: bool, gt_submittedby: lookup(systemuser), gt_completeddate: datetime }
interfaces.description: Primary graphic request table holding all metadata, status, and the generated control number.

interfaces.name: gt_sequencecounter
interfaces.signature: Entity { gt_opportunityid: string(6), gt_year: int, gt_lastorder: int, gt_compositekey: string(9) [unique: OppID+Year] }
interfaces.description: Per-opportunity-per-year monotonic counter table used by the autonumber flow to atomically allocate the next 4-digit Order.

interfaces.name: gt_statushistory
interfaces.signature: Entity { gt_graphicrequestid: lookup(gt_graphicrequest), gt_fromstatus: optionset, gt_tostatus: optionset, gt_changedby: lookup(systemuser), gt_changedon: datetime }
interfaces.description: Lightweight history log of status transitions to drive notification flow and operational diagnostics.

modules.name: Autonumber Generation Flow
modules.purpose: Server-side cloud flow that atomically allocates the next Order, composes the control number, and writes it back to the graphic request record.

interfaces.name: GraphicRequest_OnCreate
interfaces.signature: trigger: Dataverse 'When a row is added' on gt_graphicrequest; output: updates gt_graphiccontrolnumber on same row
interfaces.description: Fires synchronously after a new graphic request is created; resolves composite key, increments counter under row-lock, formats control number, and patches the source record.

interfaces.name: AllocateNextOrder
interfaces.description: Internal flow sub-routine that locates or creates the counter row for (OppID, Year) and returns the next Order value using optimistic concurrency.

interfaces.name: FormatControlNumber
interfaces.signature: FormatControlNumber(opportunityId: string, year: int, order: int) -> string
interfaces.description: Composes 'OPPID.YY.NNNN' string with zero-padding (6.2.4 digits).

modules.name: Status Notification Flow
modules.purpose: Cloud flow that listens for status changes and sends templated email to Author/SME via Outlook connector when the per-record toggle is on.

interfaces.name: GraphicRequest_OnStatusChange
interfaces.signature: trigger: Dataverse 'When a row is modified' on gt_graphicrequest, filter: gt_status changed
interfaces.description: On every status change, evaluates email toggle and recipient and dispatches the standardized email if conditions are met.

interfaces.name: SendAuthorNotification
interfaces.description: Resolves author email, fills the templated body (title, control number, author, tracker link), and sends via Office 365 Outlook connector.

modules.name: Graphic Tracker Power App
modules.purpose: Canvas app embedded in Teams providing forms, list views, status edits, and the read-only control number display.

interfaces.name: frmNewRequest
interfaces.description: Submit form that validates required fields and writes a new graphic request to Dataverse.

interfaces.name: galTrackerList
interfaces.description: Main gallery listing graphic requests scoped to user visibility with status filters.

interfaces.name: drpTeam / drpChannel
interfaces.description: Cascading dropdowns restricted to Teams/Channels the authenticated user belongs to.

interfaces.name: tglEmailToAuthor
interfaces.description: Per-record toggle 'Email status updates to Author' bound directly to the Dataverse field.

modules.name: Teams Integration
modules.purpose: Hosts the Canvas App inside Teams and provides Graph-backed Team/Channel membership data.

interfaces.name: TeamsAppManifest
interfaces.signature: manifest.json { id, packageName, staticTabs: [GraphicTracker], permissions: [identity, messageTeamMembers] }
interfaces.description: Teams app package wrapping the Power App for personal/channel tab installation.

interfaces.name: MyTeams
interfaces.description: Returns the joined Teams for the signed-in user, used to populate the Team dropdown.

interfaces.name: ChannelsOf
interfaces.description: Returns channels of the selected Team for the cascading dropdown.

modules.name: SharePoint Document Library
modules.purpose: File store for completed graphics and templates, indexed by Graphic Control Number.

interfaces.name: GraphicAssetsLibrary
interfaces.signature: Library 'GraphicAssets' with columns: GraphicControlNumber (text, indexed), OpportunityID (text), Year (number), Status (choice)
interfaces.description: Document library with metadata columns matching Dataverse for cross-linking.

interfaces.name: LinkAssetToRequest
interfaces.description: Power App control that writes a SharePoint file URL into the related Dataverse record.

modules.name: Identity & Access (Azure AD + Power Platform Roles)
modules.purpose: Authenticates users and applies security roles to Dataverse tables.

interfaces.name: SecurityRole_GraphicTrackerUser
interfaces.signature: Role { gt_graphicrequest: Create/Read/Write, gt_sequencecounter: None (system only), gt_statushistory: Read }
interfaces.description: Standard end-user role with no direct access to counter table to prevent tampering.

interfaces.name: SecurityRole_GraphicTrackerAdmin
interfaces.description: Operational admin role for support team.

modules.name: Operations & Monitoring
modules.purpose: Owns Dataverse audit configuration, flow run analytics, and licensing/ALM setup.

interfaces.name: AuditConfig
interfaces.description: Dataverse audit settings to satisfy the mitigation for risk R1.

interfaces.name: SolutionPackage
interfaces.signature: Managed solution 'GraphicTrackerAutonumber' exported with all tables, flows, and app components
interfaces.description: ALM solution package for Dev → Test → Prod promotion.

tasks.title: Create Power Platform environments and managed solution shell
tasks.description: Provision Dev, Test, and Prod Dataverse-enabled environments. Create a publisher 'gt' (prefix gt_) and a managed solution 'GraphicTrackerAutonumber' that will hold all artifacts. Confirm premium Power Platform per-user or per-app licensing with the licensing team before any builds (mitigates risk R4). Acceptance: three environments exist, solution shell created in Dev, license model documented.
tasks.pseudocode:
1. Admin Center > New environment (Dev, Test, Prod) with Dataverse enabled
2. Create publisher: name='Graphic Tracker', prefix='gt'
3. Create solution: name='GraphicTrackerAutonumber', publisher='gt'
4. Verify licensing model with Accenture MS licensing team
5. Document environment URLs and license decision in runbook

tasks.title: Design and create Dataverse tables (gt_graphicrequest, gt_sequencecounter, gt_statushistory)
tasks.description: Create the three Dataverse tables with the exact columns defined in the Dataverse Schema module. Mark gt_graphiccontrolnumber as a text column with maxlen=14 and a unique alternate key. On gt_sequencecounter, create an alternate key on (gt_opportunityid, gt_year) to enforce one counter per opportunity-year combo (mitigates risk R6 — year rollover). Acceptance: tables visible in solution, alternate keys created, columns match interface spec.
tasks.pseudocode:
Table gt_graphicrequest:
  gt_title (text, required, 200)
  gt_opportunityid (text, required, 6)
  gt_year (int, required)
  gt_order (int)
  gt_graphiccontrolnumber (text, 14, alternate key 'uk_gcn')
  gt_status (choice: Pending=1, InProgress=2, Completed=3)
  gt_emailtoauthor (yes/no, default=Yes)
  gt_authorsmeid (lookup -> systemuser)
  gt_teamid (text, 64)
  gt_channelid (text, 64)
  gt_submittedby (lookup -> systemuser)
  gt_completeddate (datetime)

Table gt_sequencecounter:
  gt_opportunityid (text, 6)
  gt_year (int)
  gt_lastorder (int, default=0)
  Alternate key 'uk_opp_year' on (gt_opportunityid, gt_year)

Table gt_statushistory:
  gt_graphicrequestid (lookup)
  gt_fromstatus, gt_tostatus (choice)
  gt_changedby (lookup)
  gt_changedon (datetime)

tasks.title: Configure Dataverse security roles and table privileges
tasks.description: Create 'GraphicTrackerUser' and 'GraphicTrackerAdmin' roles. User role: CRUD on gt_graphicrequest (user-level), Read on gt_statushistory, NO direct access to gt_sequencecounter (system-only). Admin role: full access. Bind roles to an Entra AD security group for the 262 FTEs. Acceptance: roles created in solution, end user cannot read/edit counter table directly via API.
tasks.module: Identity & Access (Azure AD + Power Platform Roles)
tasks.pseudocode:
Role 'GraphicTrackerUser':
  gt_graphicrequest: Create=User, Read=User, Write=User, Delete=None
  gt_statushistory: Read=Org
  gt_sequencecounter: NONE

Role 'GraphicTrackerAdmin':
  gt_*: Full (Org level)

Assign Azure AD group 'GT-Users' -> GraphicTrackerUser
Assign Azure AD group 'GT-Admins' -> GraphicTrackerAdmin

tasks.title: Enable Dataverse auditing on graphic request and counter tables
tasks.description: Enable environment-level auditing and table-level auditing on gt_graphicrequest (Create/Update/Delete) and gt_sequencecounter (Create/Update). This addresses the mitigation for risk R1 (audit trail gap). Acceptance: audit logs show entries after manual test inserts/updates.
tasks.pseudocode:
Settings > Audit > Enable for environment
Table gt_graphicrequest: Audit = ON (Create, Update, Delete)
Table gt_sequencecounter: Audit = ON (Create, Update)
Test: insert row, change status, verify audit history visible

tasks.title: Build Autonumber Generation cloud flow with atomic counter increment
tasks.description: Create a Power Automate flow triggered by 'When a row is added' to gt_graphicrequest. Resolve the composite key (OppID, Year). Use a Try/Catch pattern with optimistic concurrency: retrieve the counter row by alternate key; if not found, attempt to create one with lastorder=1; if found, increment lastorder using If-Match ETag header on the update call. On 412 Precondition Failed, retry up to 5 times with exponential backoff. Format the resulting control number using FormatControlNumber. Patch the gt_graphicrequest row with gt_graphiccontrolnumber, gt_order, and gt_year. This mitigates risk R3 (sequence collision).
tasks.pseudocode:
trigger: Dataverse 'When a row is added' on gt_graphicrequest
  scope: organization, run as: system user

year = mod(year(utcNow()), 100)
oppId = padLeft(triggerRow.gt_opportunityid, 6, '0')

for attempt in 1..5:
  try:
    counter = GetRowByAltKey('gt_sequencecounter', oppId+'_'+year)
    if counter == null:
      created = CreateRow('gt_sequencecounter', { oppId, year, lastorder: 1 })
      order = 1
      break
    else:
      newOrder = counter.lastorder + 1
      UpdateRow('gt_sequencecounter', counter.id,
                { lastorder: newOrder },
                headers: { 'If-Match': counter.etag })
      order = newOrder
      break
  catch HTTP 412:
    wait(2^attempt * 100ms)
    continue
  catch HTTP 409 (duplicate create):
    continue

if order is null: throw 'Counter allocation failed'

gcn = padLeft(oppId,6,'0') + '.' + padLeft(year,2,'0') + '.' + padLeft(order,4,'0')
UpdateRow('gt_graphicrequest', triggerRow.id,
          { gt_graphiccontrolnumber: gcn, gt_order: order, gt_year: year })

tasks.title: Lock down gt_graphiccontrolnumber as read-only post-generation
tasks.description: Enforce immutability of the control number at the data layer. Create a Dataverse business rule OR a synchronous workflow that prevents updates to gt_graphiccontrolnumber when its current value is non-empty. Also set the column to read-only in all forms. Acceptance: API update attempts to change a populated GCN fail; users cannot edit the field in the app.
tasks.pseudocode:
Business rule 'BR_GCN_ReadOnly':
  scope: entity
  condition: gt_graphiccontrolnumber IsNotNullOrEmpty (pre-image)
  action: set field 'gt_graphiccontrolnumber' read-only=true on form

Real-time workflow 'WF_GCN_PreventUpdate':
  trigger: on Update of gt_graphicrequest, filter gt_graphiccontrolnumber
  stage: pre-operation
  if preImage.gt_graphiccontrolnumber != null
     and preImage.gt_graphiccontrolnumber != postImage.gt_graphiccontrolnumber:
        throw InvalidPluginExecutionException('Control number is read-only')

tasks.title: Build Status Notification cloud flow with templated email
tasks.description: Create a Power Automate flow triggered by 'When a row is modified' on gt_graphicrequest filtered on gt_status changes. Guard: only proceed when triggerRow.gt_emailtoauthor == true AND gt_status == Completed (per BT email template). Look up Author/SME email via the user lookup. Compose the exact BT-provided email body substituting Title, GCN, and Author. Send via Office 365 Outlook connector. Log a row to gt_statushistory for the transition. Acceptance: status change to Completed with toggle on sends the BT email verbatim; toggle off suppresses send.
tasks.pseudocode:
trigger: Dataverse 'When a row is modified' on gt_graphicrequest
  filter columns: gt_status

if triggerRow.gt_status != Completed: terminate Succeeded

InsertRow('gt_statushistory', {
  graphicrequestid: triggerRow.id,
  fromstatus: triggerRow._old_status,
  tostatus: triggerRow.gt_status,
  changedby: triggerRow.modifiedby,
  changedon: utcNow()
})

if triggerRow.gt_emailtoauthor == false: terminate Succeeded

author = GetUser(triggerRow.gt_authorsmeid)
trackerLink = concat(envUrl, '/main.aspx?etn=gt_graphicrequest&id=', triggerRow.id)

body = template:
  Dear Author/SME,
  We are pleased to inform you that the graphic for your proposal has been completed...
  Title : {{triggerRow.gt_title}}
  Graphic Control Number : {{triggerRow.gt_graphiccontrolnumber}}
  Author/SME/Contact  : {{author.fullname}}
  Submitted By : GraphicTrackerAutonu
  ... link: {{trackerLink}} ...

Outlook.SendEmail(to=author.email,
  subject='Graphic Completed: ' + triggerRow.gt_graphiccontrolnumber,
  body=body, importance=Normal)

tasks.title: Build Canvas Power App shell with screens and navigation
tasks.description: Create the Canvas app in the solution with three screens: HomeScreen (list), NewRequestScreen (form), DetailScreen (read view + edit-status). Wire Dataverse connector to gt_graphicrequest. Acceptance: app opens, navigation works, list shows seed data.
tasks.pseudocode:
App.OnStart:
  Set(currentUser, User())
  ClearCollect(colMyRequests, Filter(gt_graphicrequest, Owner=currentUser))

HomeScreen:
  galTrackerList.Items = SortByColumns(
    Filter(gt_graphicrequest, !IsBlank(gt_graphiccontrolnumber) || true),
    'createdon', Descending)

Navigation:
  btnNew.OnSelect = Navigate(NewRequestScreen, ScreenTransition.Fade)
  galTrackerList.OnSelect = Navigate(DetailScreen, None, {req: ThisItem})
  btnBack on all sub-screens

tasks.title: Implement Team/Channel cascading dropdowns restricted to user memberships
tasks.description: Add a Microsoft Teams connector (or Graph custom connector if richer scopes needed) to the app. drpTeam populated via 'My Teams'. drpChannel populated via 'List channels' filtered by drpTeam.Selected.id. This enforces the BT business rule that Team/Channel dropdowns show only Teams/Channels the user is a member of. Acceptance: a user only sees their joined Teams; channel dropdown updates on team change.
tasks.pseudocode:
// Connector: Microsoft Teams (premium)

drpTeam.Items = MicrosoftTeams.GetAllTeams().value
drpTeam.DisplayFields = ['displayName']

drpChannel.Items =
  If(IsBlank(drpTeam.Selected),
     Blank(),
     MicrosoftTeams.GetChannelsForGroup(drpTeam.Selected.id).value)
drpChannel.DisplayFields = ['displayName']

// On submit, store teamId and channelId
frmNewRequest.OnSubmit:
  Patch(gt_graphicrequest, Defaults(gt_graphicrequest), {
    gt_teamid: drpTeam.Selected.id,
    gt_channelid: drpChannel.Selected.id, ... })

tasks.title: Build New Request form with validation and submit logic
tasks.description: Construct frmNewRequest with controls: txtTitle, txtOpportunityId (regex 6 digits), peoplePickerAuthor (Author/SME), drpTeam, drpChannel, tglEmailToAuthor (default ON). Block submit if Title or OpportunityID invalid. On submit, Patch a new gt_graphicrequest row with gt_status=Pending (this fires the autonumber flow from task 5). Show a confirmation screen with the resulting GCN once the trigger completes. Acceptance: invalid input is blocked, successful submit creates the row and GCN appears within a few seconds.
tasks.pseudocode:
txtOpportunityId.OnChange:
  Set(isOppValid, IsMatch(txtOpportunityId.Text, "\d{6}"))

btnSubmit.DisplayMode =
  If(!IsBlank(txtTitle.Text)
     && isOppValid
     && !IsBlank(peoplePickerAuthor.Selected)
     && !IsBlank(drpTeam.Selected)
     && !IsBlank(drpChannel.Selected),
     Edit, Disabled)

btnSubmit.OnSelect:
  Set(newRow, Patch(gt_graphicrequest, Defaults(gt_graphicrequest), {
    gt_title: txtTitle.Text,
    gt_opportunityid: Text(txtOpportunityId.Text),
    gt_authorsmeid: peoplePickerAuthor.Selected,
    gt_teamid: drpTeam.Selected.id,
    gt_channelid: drpChannel.Selected.id,
    gt_emailtoauthor: tglEmailToAuthor.Value,
    gt_status: 'Pending',
    gt_submittedby: currentUser
  }));
  // Poll for GCN populated by autonumber flow
  Set(polled, LookUp(gt_graphicrequest, gt_graphicrequestid = newRow.gt_graphicrequestid));
  Navigate(ConfirmationScreen, None, { gcn: polled.gt_graphiccontrolnumber })

tasks.title: Build Detail and Edit screens with read-only GCN and status editor
tasks.description: Create DetailScreen showing all fields read-only including gt_graphiccontrolnumber (DisplayMode=View). Allow inline edit of gt_status (Pending → In Progress → Completed) and gt_emailtoauthor toggle. Save via Patch. Acceptance: status changes save and trigger notification flow (task 7); users cannot modify GCN.

tasks.title: Package and deploy the Canvas app as a Teams app
tasks.description: Use Power Apps 'Add to Teams' or build a Teams app manifest with a static tab pointing to the published Power App. Submit to Accenture Teams app catalog. Acceptance: end users can install the Graphic Tracker tab in any of their Teams and launch the app inside Teams without leaving the client.
tasks.pseudocode:
1. Power Apps Studio > App > Add to Teams
2. Generate manifest.json:
   { "id": GUID,
     "packageName": "com.accenture.graphictracker",
     "name": { "short": "Graphic Tracker" },
     "staticTabs": [{ "entityId": "gt", "contentUrl": <powerapps embed url> }],
     "permissions": ["identity"]
   }
3. Zip manifest + icons -> .zip
4. Submit via Teams Admin Center > Manage apps > Upload
5. Approve and publish org-wide

tasks.title: Create SharePoint document library 'GraphicAssets' and link to Dataverse
tasks.description: Create SharePoint Online library 'GraphicAssets' with metadata columns: GraphicControlNumber (text, indexed), OpportunityID (text), Year (number), Status (choice). Add a 'Attach asset' button in the Detail screen that opens the library filtered by the current GCN. Acceptance: clicking Attach asset opens the SharePoint library filtered to the current request's GCN.
tasks.pseudocode:
// SharePoint setup
Site: PursuitServices > New library: GraphicAssets
Columns:
  GraphicControlNumber (Text, indexed=Yes)
  OpportunityID (Text)
  Year (Number)
  Status (Choice: Pending/InProgress/Completed)

// In Power App DetailScreen
btnAttachAsset.OnSelect:
  Launch(
    Concatenate(
      "https://accenture.sharepoint.com/sites/PursuitServices/GraphicAssets/Forms/AllItems.aspx?",
      "FilterField1=GraphicControlNumber&FilterValue1=",
      EncodeUrl(req.gt_graphiccontrolnumber)
    )
  )

tasks.title: Author flow-level error handling and operational alerting
tasks.description: Add Try/Catch (Scope) blocks to both Power Automate flows. On terminal failure: send email to GT-Admins distribution list with the failed record ID and error details, mark gt_status remains unchanged. Configure Power Platform admin analytics monitoring on the two flows with email alerts on >1% failure rate over 24h (addresses risk R5 mitigation). Acceptance: forcing a failure in dev triggers admin alert email.
tasks.pseudocode:
Both flows wrapped:
  Scope 'Try':
    <main logic>
  Scope 'Catch':
    runAfter: Try has failed | timedOut
    Outlook.SendEmail(
      to: 'gt-admins@accenture.com',
      subject: 'GT flow failed: ' + workflow().name,
      body: outputs('Try').error + ' record=' + triggerRow.id
    )

Power Platform Admin > Analytics > set alert:
  flow: Autonumber Generation, threshold: failure rate > 1% / 24h

tasks.title: Implement ALM pipeline: export and promote managed solution Dev → Test → Prod
tasks.description: Use Power Platform Build Tools (or Pipelines) to export the managed solution from Dev, deploy to Test for UAT, then to Prod. Include environment variables for SharePoint site URL and admin distribution list to avoid hard-coding. Acceptance: a single pipeline run promotes the solution; environment variables differ per environment but the solution package is identical.
tasks.pseudocode:
Environment variables in solution:
  ev_SharePointSiteUrl, ev_AdminDL, ev_TrackerBaseUrl

Pipeline stages:
  1. Export managed: pac solution export --managed
  2. Deploy Test: pac solution import --env=Test --settings=test.json
  3. UAT smoke test (task 17)
  4. Approval gate
  5. Deploy Prod: pac solution import --env=Prod --settings=prod.json
  6. Smoke test in Prod

tasks.title: Write unit tests for autonumber formatting and counter logic
tasks.description: Implement Power Automate Test Studio tests (or custom test harness using the Web API) for: (a) FormatControlNumber padding, (b) AllocateNextOrder first allocation, (c) AllocateNextOrder increment, (d) concurrent allocations (simulate 10 parallel inserts for same Opp/Year and assert 10 unique Orders), (e) year rollover (insert across year boundary asserts new counter row). Acceptance: all five tests pass in Dev environment.
tasks.pseudocode:
test_format_padding:
  assert format('7', 26, 1) == '000007.26.0001'
  assert format('123456', 26, 9999) == '123456.26.9999'

test_first_allocation:
  delete counter('000007', 26)
  insert request(oppId='000007')
  wait flow
  assert request.gcn == '000007.26.0001'

test_concurrent:
  parallel_for i in 1..10:
    insert request(oppId='000099')
  wait all flows
  orders = select gcn from requests where opp='000099' and year=26
  assert distinct(orders).count == 10
  assert set(orders) == {0001..0010 formatted}

test_year_rollover:
  mock utcNow = '2027-01-01'
  insert request(oppId='000007')
  assert request.gcn == '000007.27.0001'
  assert counter('000007', 27) exists with lastorder=1

tasks.title: Run end-to-end UAT scenarios and capture sign-off
tasks.description: Execute end-to-end scenarios in Test: (1) Submitter creates request → GCN generated → designer sees it → designer changes status to In Progress → toggle on → status to Completed → author receives email with exact BT template → SharePoint asset attached. (2) Repeat with toggle off → no email sent. (3) Submit with malformed Opp ID → blocked at form. (4) User attempts to edit GCN via Web API → blocked with error. Document results and obtain BT sign-off.
tasks.pseudocode:
Scenarios:
  S1 Happy path: create -> generate -> in-progress -> completed -> email received
  S2 Email off: create with toggle=off -> completed -> NO email
  S3 Validation: submit oppId='ABC' -> form rejects
  S4 Immutability: PATCH /gt_graphicrequests(id) {gt_graphiccontrolnumber:'X'} -> 400/forbidden
  S5 Membership: user A sees only their Teams in dropdown
  S6 Volume: bulk insert 1000 requests over 1 hour -> all unique GCNs, no duplicates

Deliverable: signed UAT report from BT

testStrategy.unit: Use Power Automate Test Studio and Power Apps Test Studio to define repeatable tests at the function/screen level: FormatControlNumber padding, counter increment branches, business rule blocking GCN updates, and dropdown filtering by user membership. Each cloud flow ships with at least one Test Studio test that mocks the Dataverse trigger payload and asserts the side-effect record state via the Web API. Run tests on every solution import via the ALM pipeline (task 15).
testStrategy.integration: End-to-end Dataverse integration tests run in the Test environment using a service-principal-driven Web API harness: create requests in bulk, assert the Autonumber Generation Flow has populated GCN and gt_order, and assert the Status Notification Flow inserts statushistory rows and emits emails (capture via a shared mailbox). Exercise the cascading Team/Channel behavior with two test users in different Teams to validate the membership scope. Validate the full SharePoint linkage by uploading a file tagged with a GCN and confirming the Detail screen launches the filtered library view.
edgeCases: 10 concurrent submissions against the same Opportunity ID and Year — must produce 10 distinct Orders (0001..0010) with no gaps or duplicates.
Year boundary crossing — a submission at 2026-12-31 23:59:59 UTC and one at 2027-01-01 00:00:01 UTC for the same OppID must produce separate counters and the 2027 record must restart at 0001.
Opportunity ID with fewer than 6 digits — form-level validation must block submit; if bypassed via API, the flow must left-pad with zeros to length 6.
Order reaching 9999 — the next submission for the same Opp/Year must surface a clear error (BT to confirm desired behavior; see open question Q1).
User toggles 'Email status updates to Author' OFF then changes status to Completed — flow must not send the email.
User toggles ON, status changes from Pending → In Progress (not Completed) — current spec only emails on Completed; verify with BT that intermediate transitions are not notified.
Attempted update of gt_graphiccontrolnumber via Web API after it has been populated — must be rejected by the real-time workflow.
Power Automate platform outage during submission — request row persists with empty GCN; on platform restore, a recovery job (or manual replay) must complete the autonumber assignment.
User is a member of zero Teams — drpTeam shows empty, form must still allow free-text fallback or block with friendly message (BT to confirm).
Author lookup user has been deactivated in Entra AD — notification flow must catch the resolve failure and route the alert to GT-Admins instead of throwing.

openQuestions.question: What is the desired behavior when the 4-digit Order counter reaches 9999 for an Opportunity in a given year? Roll to alphanumeric, error, or extend to 5 digits?

Should the Status Notification email fire on intermediate status transitions (Pending → In Progress) or only on Completed? BT's provided template is specific to Completed; intermediate states are unclear.
For modified/revised graphics, does each modification create a new gt_graphicrequest row (and therefore a new GCN), or does it update the existing row? Risk R2 in the SDD flagged this as unresolved.
Is a Teams membership of zero a valid state (no joined Teams), and if so, should the form block submit, allow free-text Team/Channel, or fall back to a default Pursuit Services Team?
Confirm the exact subject line for the BT-provided email body — the body is specified but the subject is not. Proposed default: 'Graphic Completed: <GCN>'.
Confirm whether the per-app Power Platform license is sufficient (one app, ~300 users) or whether per-user premium licenses are required by other concurrent Power Platform usage at Accenture.
